pending.body: In `record_planned_elsewhere_round_sentinel` (service.rs ~line 2407), `Utc::now()` is called directly for the event timestamp instead of accepting a `now: DateTime<Utc>` parameter like every other event-writing function in this module. This breaks the testability pattern used throughout the codebase (e.g., `reconcile_planned_elsewhere` accepts `now`, `record_planned_elsewhere_mapping` uses `mapping.recorded_at`). Change the signature to accept a `now` parameter and pass it from `record_planned_elsewhere_amendments` in engine.rs, which already has `Utc::now()` in scope.

Fix `load_planned_elsewhere_mappings()` so a newer run can supersede mappings from an abandoned run. `PlannedElsewhereMapping.run_id` is documented as the way to ignore stale mappings from rolled-back or abandoned runs (`src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs:747-749`), but `rebuild_planned_elsewhere_from_journal()` still computes max rounds per `(active_bead_id, run_id)` and then keeps the latest mapping from every run (`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2583-2611`). If bead `A` records a planned-elsewhere mapping in `run-1` and a later `run-2` completes with no planned-elsewhere findings, `load_planned_elsewhere_mappings()` will still return the stale `run-1` mapping forever. Collapse milestone state to the latest authoritative run per bead, or make the loader require the authoritative run id instead of exposing superseded cross-run data as current state.

Do not silently discard failures from `record_planned_elsewhere_round_sentinel()`. Both completion paths call it and ignore the result (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:7617-7624` and `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:3088-3094` via `record_planned_elsewhere_amendments()`), but the zero-PE supersession logic in `rebuild_planned_elsewhere_from_journal()` depends on those sentinel journal entries (`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2496-2526` and `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2590-2608`). A journal append failure in a later round with zero planned-elsewhere findings will therefore silently resurrect stale mappings, and today there is no warning or retry path for operators to notice.

status_summary: running: final review restart round 22 -> Plan and Implement
